channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tesla's making money. But it's planning to spend an awful lot more
item.description: Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.
item.content:encoded:
Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Family influencers make the lifestyle look good. But kids pay the price, new book says
item.description: What does it mean to monetize your offspring? To turn their childhood into content? In Like, Follow, Subscribe Fortesa Latifi explores what drives parents to become family influencers.
item.content:encoded: What does it mean to monetize your offspring? To turn their childhood into content? In Like, Follow, Subscribe Fortesa Latifi explores what drives parents to become family influencers.
item.dc:creator: Ilana Masad

item.title: 'We can do better,' FAA head says of work to update U.S. analog air traffic system
item.description: The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: What having a product guy as Apple's CEO might mean for the company
item.description: Apple's next CEO has been responsible for developing the hardware for many products. NPR's Juana Summers talks to Jay Peters of The Verge about what the company could look like under his leadership.
item.content:encoded: Apple's next CEO has been responsible for developing the hardware for many products. NPR's Juana Summers talks to Jay Peters of The Verge about what the company could look like under his leadership.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: Florida AG launches criminal investigation into ChatGPT over FSU shooting
item.description: Florida's attorney general is launching a criminal investigation into the alleged role of ChatGPT in a mass shooting at Florida State University last year.
item.content:encoded:
Florida's attorney general is launching a criminal investigation into the alleged role of ChatGPT in a mass shooting at Florida State University last year.

(Image credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo)
item.dc:creator: Tristan Wood

item.title: She raised concerns about her company's contracts with ICE. Then she lost her job
item.description: Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.
item.content:encoded:
Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.

(Image credit: Octavio Jones)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: The surprising origin of 4 features that superglue kids — and adults — to screens
item.description: Taken together, these four features can create a trancelike state that can keep us stuck on social media apps or video games for hours. Children are particularly vulnerable.
item.content:encoded:
Taken together, these four features can create a trancelike state that can keep us stuck on social media apps or video games for hours. Children are particularly vulnerable.

(Image credit: Paige Stampatori for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Michaeleen Doucleff

item.title: Apple's Tim Cook to step down as CEO
item.description: Tim Cook says he'll step down as Apple's CEO in September, becoming the company's executive chairman. He will be replaced by John Ternus, Apple's senior vice president of hardware engineering.
item.content:encoded: Tim Cook says he'll step down as Apple's CEO in September, becoming the company's executive chairman. He will be replaced by John Ternus, Apple's senior vice president of hardware engineering.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
item.description: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.
item.content:encoded:
The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: These robots can figure out how to do a task after watching humans do it
item.description: Swiss scientists have published research showing AI-informed robots can learn how to self correct and teach other robots how to behave. It raises questions of consciousness in artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded: Swiss scientists have published research showing AI-informed robots can learn how to self correct and teach other robots how to behave. It raises questions of consciousness in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle
